finalJson.description:
Join a dedicated team at a premier health and rehabilitation center in Fairhope, AL, as a Full-time AM Staff Nurse specializing in medication administration. This is an excellent opportunity for a compassionate and skilled Registered Nurse or Licensed Practical Nurse to make a significant impact on resident care during morning shifts.

We are thrilled to announce a brand new Sign-On and Travel/Stipend Welcome Program designed to attract high-quality nursing professionals. Registered Nurses (RNs) are eligible for a total of $12,000 ($6,000 sign-on bonus + $6,000 travel/stipend), while Licensed Practical Nurses (LPNs) can receive $6,000 total ($3,000 sign-on bonus + $3,000 travel/stipend). Payments are distributed weekly after the first 30 days of employment, contingent on maintaining a strong performance record.

Candidates should demonstrate competency, a strong work ethic, and trustworthiness, with the ability to accurately perform all assigned duties related to medication management and resident care. This program is applicable to team members who have been out of the facility for 6 months or greater.
requirements: Active and valid Registered Nurse (RN) or Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN) license in Alabama
Previous experience in a skilled nursing facility or long-term care setting preferred
Demonstrated competency in medication administration and patient care
Strong work ethic, trustworthiness, and ability to perform assigned duties
Ability to maintain a record of no call-offs, no tardies, and zero disciplinary actions to remain eligible for bonus
finalJson.salaryOrBonusInfo: Exciting new Sign-On and Travel/Stipend Welcome Program! Registered Nurses (RNs) are eligible for $12,000 total ($6,000 sign-on bonus + $6,000 travel/stipend). Licensed Practical Nurses (LPNs) are eligible for $6,000 total ($3,000 sign-on bonus + $3,000 travel/stipend). Payments will be distributed weekly after the first 30 days of employment (after completion of onboarding and orientation). Employees must maintain a record of no call-offs, no tardies, and zero disciplinary actions to remain eligible each month. Applicable to team members who have been out of the facility for 6 months or greater.

Applicable to team members who have been out of the facility for 6 months or greater.

finalJson.description:
We are seeking a dedicated and experienced Registered Nurse (RN) MDS Coordinator to join a premier health and rehabilitation center in Vernon, AL. In this vital role, you will be responsible for the timely and accurate completion of the Minimum Data Set (MDS) assessments, ensuring compliance with federal and state regulations, and contributing to comprehensive care planning for residents. This position offers an opportunity to make a significant impact on resident care and facility operations.

The ideal candidate will possess a strong understanding of the Resident Assessment Instrument (RAI) process and MDS 3.0. You will collaborate closely with the interdisciplinary team to ensure that assessments accurately reflect the resident's condition and care needs. If you are a detail-oriented RN with a passion for quality care and regulatory compliance, we encourage you to apply.
requirements: Active Registered Nurse (RN) license in Alabama
Minimum of 1-2 years of experience as an MDS Coordinator
Proficiency with MDS 3.0 and the Resident Assessment Instrument (RAI) process
Strong understanding of federal and state regulations related to long-term care
Excellent organizational and communication skills
